title: corylus avellana f. glomerata
intro: Corylus avellana f. glomerata, also known as the Hazel or Filbert, is a variety of the Hazel tree that has a compact, shrub-like growth habit and produces edible nuts. Traditionally, the nuts, leaves, and bark of Corylus avellana f. glomerata have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Corylus avellana f. glomerata include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, as well as acting as a natural diuretic and antiseptic. In herbal preparations, Corylus avellana f. glomerata is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
